text: Trump declares 'NO MORE' Israeli strikes on South Pars but threatens US will 'massively blow up' the entire field if Iran hits Qatar again. Axios: Trump DID coordinate original strike with Netanyahu.
Ras Laffan struck SECOND time in 12 hours — QatarEnergy reports 'sizeable fires and extensive further damage' to multiple LNG facilities. Qatar's LNG output halted since March 2.
Iran fires 6+ ballistic missile salvos at Israel overnight with cluster warheads. Foreign worker killed at Moshav Adanim. 5 injured in Jaljulia direct hit. IRGC framing as sustained 'punishment.'
Asian markets crash Thursday open — Nikkei -2.7%, KOSPI -2.6%, Hang Seng -1.4%. Brent breaks $110. Dubai crude hits all-time high $150+ for Asian buyers. Goldman revising target to $130.
FBI investigating Joe Kent for classified leaks. Saudi FM: 'trust in Iran completely shattered,' reserves right to military action. 12-nation Riyadh statement demands Iran halt attacks.
Confirmed: IDF struck Bandar Anzali naval base on the Caspian Sea — first-ever Israeli strike in northern Iran. 4 missile boats and corvette destroyed. Targeting Iran-Russia supply lifeline.

BREAKING: Iran has now struck energy infrastructure in ALL 6 GCC countries simultaneously. Ras Laffan extensive damage, SAMREF in Yanbu hit, 2 Kuwaiti refineries ablaze, Habshan shut down. Unprecedented regional escalation.

Satellite imagery shows 4 gas treatment plants at South Pars/Asaluyeh completely offline. Major fires visible from space. Iran's gas output likely cut by 30-40% — will affect domestic heating and electricity.

Pentagon $200B supplemental is extraordinary — more than every country's annual defense budget except the US itself. Combined with existing $1T budget, US military spending approaches $1.2T annualized.
IMO talks on safe maritime corridor started yesterday — first formal attempt to reopen Hormuz. Only 21 tankers have transited since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. 500+ tankers still trapped in the Gulf.

DNI Gabbard testimony bombshell: Iran's nuclear enrichment was 'obliterated' in June 2025 and they weren't rebuilding. Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed 'severely injured.' This contradicts the administration's stated war rationale.

Qatar has expelled Iran's military and security attaches, giving them 24 hours to leave. Saudi Arabia says trust in Iran is 'completely shattered.' Gulf states turning hostile to Iran despite opposing the US/Israeli war.

Netanyahu evening press conference confirms Israel acted alone on South Pars strike. Claims Iran 'can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles' — provides no evidence. IAEA chief Grossi contradicts: 'the material will still be there.'
verificationNote: Press conference livestreamed; IAEA statement separately confirmed

text: House Intelligence Committee hearing: Gabbard again dodges imminent nuclear threat question. Democrats pressing all three (Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel) on war justification. Separate hearing from March 18 Senate bombshell.
verificationNote: NBC and ABC confirmed hearing details

text: Gen. Caine confirms 5,000-lb penetrators used on underground storage sites. 120+ Iranian naval vessels sunk. A-10s actively hunting boats in Hormuz. Iran's surface fleet 'no longer a factor.' 11 submarines eliminated.
verificationNote: Pentagon briefing confirmed by CBS, CNBC

text: UNSC Resolution 2817 passes 13-0-2 — first UN condemnation of Iran's attacks on Gulf neighbors. Russia and China abstain. France calls Iran 'primarily responsible for escalation.' Major diplomatic milestone.

QatarEnergy CEO confirms Iranian strikes knocked out 17% of Qatar's LNG export capacity. $20B annual revenue lost. 2 of 14 LNG trains damaged — repairs sideline 12.8M tons/year for 3-5 years. Europe and Asia scrambling for replacement.
verificationNote: QatarEnergy official statement; The National confirmed details

text: First hostile hit on an F-35 in history: USAF F-35 forced emergency landing after suspected Iranian fire during combat mission. CENTCOM confirms pilot safe, aircraft damaged. Investigation underway.

Brent crude briefly hits $119/barrel before settling at $108.65 after Netanyahu says Israel will help reopen Hormuz. Analysts at Wood Mackenzie warn $150-$200/barrel 'not outside the realm of possibility.' Goldman Sachs: 16.1M bpd effectively cut.
verificationNote: CNBC, Yahoo Finance confirmed market data

text: IDF confirmed first-ever strikes on Bandar Anzali, Iran's Caspian Sea port. Five missile boats destroyed along with a naval headquarters and shipyard. Represents deepest geographic reach of the air campaign so far.
verificationNote: IDF spokesperson confirmation; Alma Research Center daily report

text: Pentagon briefing reveals US has struck 7,800+ targets in Iran. All 11 subs and 120+ warships destroyed. Hegseth announced 'largest strike package yet' coming today — $200B supplemental requested from Congress.
verificationNote: Pentagon briefing aired live, CBS News confirmed

text: Dubai crude hits all-time record $166.80/barrel — physical oil market now pricing far above futures benchmarks. European jet fuel at $220/bbl, diesel at $200. Goldman warns Brent could surpass 2008 all-time high of $147.50.
verificationNote: US News, CNBC confirmed market data

text: IAF confirmed choosing not to intercept some Iranian cluster submunitions to conserve interceptor stockpile. ~50% of Iranian missiles carry cluster warheads with 24 bomblets each. 90% interception rate for populated areas maintained.
verificationNote: Times of Israel reporting with IDF official sources

text: Qatar expels Iranian military and security personnel within 24 hours after Ras Laffan LNG strike. First Gulf state to take such action since war began. Qatar FM calls it 'flagrant violation of sovereignty.'
verificationNote: Qatar MFA official statement confirmed by multiple outlets

text: Iran executes three men in Qom linked to January protests, including 19-year-old wrestler whose case drew US criticism. Rights groups describe trials as shams. First public hangings tied to the protest crackdown.
verificationNote: Confirmed by ABC News, rights organizations

text: IMO announces negotiations for humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf by Hormuz blockade. 7-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, FR, DE, IT, NL, JP, CA) issues joint statement calling for safe passage and infrastructure moratorium.
verificationNote: GOV.UK joint statement published; CNN confirmed IMO move
